title: linaria lanigera var. dealbata
intro: Linaria lanigera var. dealbata, also known as woolly toadflax or white toadflax, is a perennial plant in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Linaria lanigera var. dealbata include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic to treat wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
